subject: Re: Publicis
body:
yes as well as ghisaline

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I had a tough conversation with Sondra Thorson on
> Wednesday night during  
> which she confided that, after Publicis sent WJC
> Foundation Publicis'  latest 
> version of the CGI comprehensive financial report
> and Publicis answered  whatever 
> questions WJCF raised, Bruce Lindsey left a message
> essentially  informing 
> Publicis that the WJCF would "see Publicis in
> court".  Sondra  claimed that BL 
> would not acknowledge awareness of Forums and that
> BL, MM (who  was a Publicis 
> employee, but is now a Foundation employee), and
> WJCF, in  general, put Sondra 
> and Publicis in an unnecessarily defensive posture. 
> 
>  
> Sondra capitulated and sent me the financial reports
> Publicis sent to Bruce  
> Lindsey and agreed to give me the contact
> information of the person at 
> Publicis  who Rich can speak with to go over the
> numbers and do whatever other  
> auditing/questioning is required.  
>  
> I received the financial report yesterday and
> forwarded it to Rich.   II 
> received the contact info for Publicis' accounting
> person today and also  
> forwarded that info to Rich, so that Rich can dig
> in.  I will update  you when I have 
> anything concrete to report
>  
> In addition, although still maintaining that there
> was a basis for Publicis  
> to charge the $250,000 production fee for the CGI
> event, Sondra stated that 
> she  understood why we maintain that none should
> have been paid.  She knows at  
> the very least that this is going to be a matter of
> controversy to be 
> resolved,  but suggested that Rich finish his
> analysis first, so that we address all 
> the  issues at once and are not fighting in
> piece-meal.
>  
> I specifically asked Sondra whether Richard Attias,
> who, as you know, did  
> absolutely nothing for the CGI event, was deriving
> any money from this deal and  
> Sondra said that she believed Richard was getting
> some money, but Sondra 
> claimed  not to know how much (this may be the
> source of any aggressive accounting 
> that  we might uncover).  In addition, Sondra claims
> that Publicis told her 
> that  the "profit" that Publicis is making on CGI is
> far less than it normally 
> makes  on similar events.  I responded to her that
> the $220K that Forums made 
> on  this event was far less than the $2MM it was
> expecting to receive.
>  
> I asked Sondra if Publicis was ever out of pocket in
> connection with the  CGI 
> event.  Sondra said that she thought that Publicis
> must have been, but  she 
> could not tell me when, for how long or for how
> much.
>  
> I also asked Sondra where she thought WJCF
> suspicions and adverse posture  
> were coming from.  Sondra said that, in part, Mary
> Morrison was behind much  of 
> the questioning taking place.  In response, I told
> Sondra that it gives  me 
> great reason for concern to learn that a former
> Publicis employee for  the CGI 
> event, who was intimately involved from the
> beginning in  all aspects of the 
> planning, funding, costs and implementation of  the
> event, is now questioning 
> the financial reports of Publicis.  Sondra  claimed
> that Mary could have her own 
> personal reasons for wanting  to go after Publicis
> and Sondra maintained her 
> belief that  the entries in the reports she sent to
> BL and now to me are 
> accurate.
>  
> Would it be ok if I have Rich speak with Mary 
> Morrison at WJCF to find out 
> what her concerns are? 
>  
> Darren
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
yes as well as ghisaline

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I had a tough conversation with Sondra Thorson on
Wednesday night during
which she confided that, after Publicis sent WJC
Foundation Publicis' latest
version of the CGI comprehensive financial report
and Publicis answered whatever
questions WJCF raised, Bruce Lindsey left a message
essentially informing
Publicis that the WJCF would "see Publicis in
court". Sondra claimed that BL
would not acknowledge awareness of Forums and that
BL, MM (who was a Publicis
employee, but is now a Foundation employee), and
WJCF, in general, put Sondra
and Publicis in an unnecessarily defensive posture.

Sondra capitulated and sent me the financial reports
Publicis sent to Bruce
Lindsey and agreed to give me the contact
information of the person at
Publicis who Rich can speak with to go over the
numbers and do whatever other
auditing/questioning is required.

I received the financial report yesterday and
forwarded it to Rich. II
received the contact info for Publicis' accounting
person today and also
forwarded that info to Rich, so that Rich can dig
in. I will update you when I have
anything concrete to report

In addition, although still maintaining that there
was a basis for Publicis
to charge the $250,000 production fee for the CGI
event, Sondra stated that
she understood why we maintain that none should
have been paid. She knows at
the very least that this is going to be a matter of
controversy to be
resolved, but suggested that Rich finish his
analysis first, so that we address all
the issues at once and are not fighting in
piece-meal.

I specifically asked Sondra whether Richard Attias,
who, as you know, did
absolutely nothing for the CGI event, was deriving
any money from this deal and
Sondra said that she believed Richard was getting
some money, but Sondra
claimed not to know how much (this may be the
source of any aggressive accounting
that we might uncover). In addition, Sondra claims
that Publicis told her
that the "profit" that Publicis is making on CGI is
far less than it normally
makes on similar events. I responded to her that
the $220K that Forums made
on this event was far less than the $2MM it was
expecting to receive.

I asked Sondra if Publicis was ever out of pocket in
connection with the CGI
event. Sondra said that she thought that Publicis
must have been, but she
could not tell me when, for how long or for how
much.

I also asked Sondra where she thought WJCF
suspicions and adverse posture
were coming from. Sondra said that, in part, Mary
Morrison was behind much of
the questioning taking place. In response, I told
Sondra that it gives me
great reason for concern to learn that a former
Publicis employee for the CGI
event, who was intimately involved from the
beginning in all aspects of the
planning, funding, costs and implementation of the
event, is now questioning
the financial reports of Publicis. Sondra claimed
that Mary could have her own
personal reasons for wanting to go after Publicis
and Sondra maintained her
belief that the entries in the reports she sent to
BL and now to me are
accurate.

Would it be ok if I have Rich speak with Mary
Morrison at WJCF to find out
what her concerns are?

Darren
